name: Celebration Rugelach- Country Living
recipeIngredient: Fresh ivy or other green leaves (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pastry in two batches: In a large bowl, combine half the flour and baking powder; mix well. With an electric mixer on low speed, beat in half the butter and cream cheese until smooth dough forms. Divide the dough into 4 balls. Repeat with the remaining flour, baking powder, butter, and cream cheese to make 4 more balls of dough. Wrap each ball of dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
2. On a lightly floured surface, roll one ball of dough into a 12 by 10 inch rectangle, about 1/8 inch thick. With a fluted pastry wheel or knife, cut the rectangle into two 12 by 5-inch strips. Using a pastry wheel, trim 1/16 inch from the outside lengthwise edges to flute. Transfer the strips to a baking sheet or large plate, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate. Repeat rolling and cutting with the remaining balls of dough, stacking the strips between plastic wrap and refrigerating them as they are made.
3. Prepare the filling: Set aside 1/4 cup each of nuts and raisins. In a food processor fitted with a chopping blade, process the remaining cups walnuts until finely chopped. In a medium bowl, combine the finely chopped nuts, sugar, and cinnamon. Set the mixture aside.
4. Preheat oven to 325°F. Line one large baking sheet with aluminum foil; place two sheets of foil crosswise on another large baking sheet. Place one 9-inch pastry ring (see Note) on the first baking sheet; place a 6-inch pastry ring and a 3-inch pastry ring on separate sheets of foil on the other baking sheet. Using the tip of a skewer or toothpick, score the outline of all rings into the foil; remove the rings and set aside until ready to bake rugelach.
5. To make the 9-inch rugelach, brush one strip of dough lightly with raspberry preserves. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons walnut mixture and 1 tablespoon raisins to cover half of the strip lengthwise. Fold the uncovered half of the dough strip lengthwise over the covered half to enclose the filling and make a 12 by 2-1/2-inch strip. Brush the top of the folded strip lightly with raspberry preserves. From either end, loosely roll up the filled strip; stand, cut edges up, in the center of the 9-inch ring scored on the baking sheet.
6. Fill and fold 8 more strips of dough as in step 5; brush the top of each with preserves. With the preserve-brushed surface facing the center, wrap one filled dough strip around the rolled strip on the baking sheet. Continue wrapping the remaining 7 filled strips around the center, pinching the ends of the strips together, until the ring is filled. Sprinkle the outside of the rugelach with about 2 tablespoons walnut mixture and place a pastry ring around the rugelach spiral.
7. With the remaining pastry strips, prepare 6-inch and 3-inch rugelachs repeating steps 5 and 6, using 5 filled strips of dough for the 6-inch rugelach and 2 filled strips for the 3-inch rugelach. Pat some of the remaining walnut mixture around the outside of the rugelachs, using about 1 1/2 tablespoons on each. Carefully place pastry rings around the rugelachs.
8. Bake the 9-inch rugelach for 1 1/2 to 1 3/4 hours, the 6-inch rugelach for 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 hours, and the 3-inch rugelach for 1 hour or until the center is browned and firm to touch. Using a large spatula, remove the 3-inch rugelach, still on foil, to a wire rack, allowing the 6-inch rugelach to remain in the oven until it is completely baked. When baked, carefully transfer all rugelachs to a wire rack; remove the pastry ring and foil. Let the rugelachs cool to room temperature.
9. Just before serving, melt apricot preserves in a small saucepan and brush over the top of each rugelach. Place the 9-inch rugelach on a serving plate; top with the 6-inch and 3-inch rugelachs. If necessary, trim the tops of the 9- and 6-inch rugelachs to make them level so that tiers will stand securely. Garnish the plate with fresh ivy or other green leaves, if desired.
10. To serve, cut the 3-inch rugelach into 6 wedges and remove to individual plates. Then cut the 6-inch rugelach into 10 wedges and remove to plates; cut the 9-inch rugelach into 14 wedges and remove.
